author: @chongwingkong2043
text:
Interesting take in the video. While I get the frustration with copyright issues, it's also true that companies like HBO and Naughty Dog have a right to protect their IP. Fair use is tricky, and what one person sees as commentary, a company might see as infringing on their work or harming its market, especially with leaks. Issuing claims is often just their standard way of addressing it, and there's usually an appeal process.
On "The Last of Us" show, while the presenter isn't interested, it's hard to ignore the massive critical acclaim and positive viewer response it received. Many feel it's a high-quality adaptation that respected the source material while also expanding on it in meaningful ways, so dismissing it outright might mean missing out. And when it comes to creators seeming "sensitive," I think sometimes that's just passion for their work, or them defending against what they see as unfair attacks or bad-faith criticism, which is different from just brushing off all feedback.
As for the points praised, while it's good the presenter supports The Critical Drinker, I think it's fair to say his style of criticism isn't for everyone and can sometimes come across as overly negative or contributing to a more toxic side of fandom for some. And with Elon Musk reinstating accounts on X, while that was a positive for the presenter, a lot of people have valid concerns about the broader changes to content moderation on the platform and whether it's led to more misinformation or a less safe environment overall.
Just some different perspectives to consider on these complex topics!
